mission.title: kcp: Rename WorkloadCluster to SyncTarget
mission.description: Rename WorkloadCluster to SyncTarget. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kcp deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kcp version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
helm list -n kcp 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kcp installation.

steps.title: Review kcp configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kcp configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
kubectl get configmap -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kcp
```
WorkloadCluster is a bit misleading and has some overlap with other APIs out there, naming wise. A more appropriate name is something like SyncTarget, because the only thing we're using the API for is as a scheduling target.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Rename WorkloadCluster to SyncTarget
steps.description:
let's move forward with SyncTarget, we can change it if we need to.  

Are you able to work on this in 0.6?

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
kubectl get events -n kcp --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Rename WorkloadCluster to SyncTarget" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
let's move forward with SyncTarget, we can change it if we need to.  

Are you able to work on this in 0.6?

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kcp installed or the issue environment reproducible.
